mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean excess returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition produces negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates resilient manufacturing output that supports earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive return signal reverses to negative during late-cycle expansions, or the 20-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth drops below 0.05 in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, which mechanically suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or the predictive Rank IC is consistently negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold condition yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the signal's out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor market improvement signal generates negative 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply chain fragility that accelerates risk-off flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, or the conditional mean return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
